description:
**Bug Report**

When multiple PRs merge in quick succession, agents start executing in worktrees that are based on stale `origin/main`. The agent writes code against outdated API signatures, causing build failures and manual rebase recovery work.

**Example:** PR #1013 agent wrote routes against a KnowledgeStoreService stub because the worktree was created before PR #1012 (which added the real service) merged. Required full soft-reset and manual rewrite.

**Root Cause**

Worktrees are created via `git worktree add` from whatever commit `main` points to at creation time. There is no `git fetch && git rebase origin/main` step before agent execution begins. When main moves forward (via merged PRs), existing worktrees become stale.

**Fix**

Add a pre-execution rebase step in the agent launch pipeline. Before the Claude SDK agent starts working in a worktree:

1. `git fetch origin` — get latest remote state
2. `git rebase origin/main` — bring worktree up to date
3. If rebase fails (conflict), abort and log warning — the agent can still proceed on the stale base, but at least we tried

The ideal location is in `apps/server/src/providers/claude-agent-sdk-provider.ts` or the worktree setup in `@automaker/git-utils`, right before agent execution starts.

**Files to Modify:**
- `apps/server/src/providers/claude-agent-sdk-provider.ts` (or the worktree setup path)
- Possibly `libs/git-utils/src/` for a reusable `rebaseWorktreeOnMain()` utility

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Before agent execution, worktree is rebased onto latest `origin/main`
- [ ] If rebase fails (conflict), abort rebase cleanly and log warning — don't crash the agent
- [ ] Rebase is a no-op if worktree is already up to date
- [ ] `npm run build:server` passes
- [ ] `npm run build:packages` passes (if git-utils touched)
title: Bug: Agents execute against stale main — no pre-execution rebase

descriptionHistory.description:
**Bug Report**

When multiple PRs merge in quick succession, agents start executing in worktrees that are based on stale `origin/main`. The agent writes code against outdated API signatures, causing build failures and manual rebase recovery work.

**Example:** PR #1013 agent wrote routes against a KnowledgeStoreService stub because the worktree was created before PR #1012 (which added the real service) merged. Required full soft-reset and manual rewrite.

**Root Cause**

Worktrees are created via `git worktree add` from whatever commit `main` points to at creation time. There is no `git fetch && git rebase origin/main` step before agent execution begins. When main moves forward (via merged PRs), existing worktrees become stale.

**Fix**

Add a pre-execution rebase step in the agent launch pipeline. Before the Claude SDK agent starts working in a worktree:

1. `git fetch origin` — get latest remote state
2. `git rebase origin/main` — bring worktree up to date
3. If rebase fails (conflict), abort and log warning — the agent can still proceed on the stale base, but at least we tried

The ideal location is in `apps/server/src/providers/claude-agent-sdk-provider.ts` or the worktree setup in `@automaker/git-utils`, right before agent execution starts.

**Files to Modify:**
- `apps/server/src/providers/claude-agent-sdk-provider.ts` (or the worktree setup path)
- Possibly `libs/git-utils/src/` for a reusable `rebaseWorktreeOnMain()` utility

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Before agent execution, worktree is rebased onto latest `origin/main`
- [ ] If rebase fails (conflict), abort rebase cleanly and log warning — don't crash the agent
- [ ] Rebase is a no-op if worktree is already up to date
